review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Type definition modules import from backend, data, and router layers

evidence: typedef/common.py:2 imports DEFAULT_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS from backend.constants
typedef/runs/run_create_base.py:25 imports from backend.constants, :29 from backend.converters, :32 from data.crud_assistant, :33 from routers.supabase_session
typedef/threads/thread_create.py:12 imports from data.crud_message, :13 from data.crud_thread, :14 from routers.supabase_session
typedef/assistants/assistant_types.py:17 imports from backend.rag.index, :19 from data.crud_vector_store, :20 from routers.supabase_session
detail.suggestion: Move constants to a top-level constants module or into typedef. Extract Session into a shared dependencies/ or core/ module. Remove data access and backend logic imports from typedef by moving validation logic into routers or services. Type definitions should be the bottom layer with no upstream dependencies.

evidence: backend/composer.py:37 imports chat_complete and chat_complete_stream_raw from routers.openai.chat
detail.suggestion: Extract the chat completion logic into a shared service module under backend/ (e.g., backend/chat_service.py) that both composer.py and routers/openai/chat.py can depend on. Backend modules should never import from routers.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Data layer modules import from backend security and constants

evidence: data/crud_api_key.py:8 imports APIKey and KEY_PREFIX from backend.security.api_key, :9 imports THIRTY_DAYS_SECONDS from backend.constants
data/crud_vector_content.py:7 imports TOP_K from backend.constants
detail.suggestion: Move shared constants (TOP_K, THIRTY_DAYS_SECONDS) to a neutral location such as a top-level constants module or into the data layer if they are data concerns. Move APIKey validation utilities into a shared security module that sits below or alongside the data layer.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: routers/supabase_session.py is imported by 23 files across all architectural layers

evidence: supabase_session.py is imported by typedef modules (run_create_base.py, thread_create.py, message_types.py, assistant_types.py, thread_run_create_params_request.py, run_create.py)
supabase_session.py is imported by backend module composer.py
supabase_session.py is imported by all router modules in routers/openai/ and routers/cowabunga/
detail.suggestion: Extract the Session dependency and authentication utilities into a shared core/dependencies/ module that is independent of routers. This decouples the session type from the router layer and allows all layers to depend on it without creating layer violations.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: backend/composer.py is a 403-line hub importing from nearly every module

evidence: composer.py imports from backend.converters, backend.rag.query, data.crud_assistant, data.crud_message, routers.openai.chat, routers.supabase_session, utils.config, cowabunga_sdk.chat.chat_pb2, and multiple typedef modules
composer.py has a re-export ratio of 0.98 but also contains substantial orchestration logic
detail.suggestion: Split composer.py into domain-specific service modules (e.g., chat_service.py, thread_service.py, run_service.py) each owning a cohesive slice of orchestration. Keep composer.py only as a thin facade if necessary, or remove it entirely in favor of direct service usage.

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Committed build artifacts duplicate cowabunga_sdk source inside build/lib/

evidence: src/cowabunga_sdk/build/lib/cowabunga_sdk/ contains full copies of __init__.py, llm.py, serve.py, utils.py, cli.py, errors.py, config.py and all protobuf modules
These files are stale copies of source files already present at the package root
detail.suggestion: Delete src/cowabunga_sdk/build/ entirely and add it to .gitignore. Build artifacts should never be committed to source control.

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::unte.summary: Core backend orchestration and RAG modules have zero test coverage

evidence: composer.py is 403 loc with no unit or integration tests
rag/index.py, query.py, document_loader.py, and leapfrogai_embeddings.py have no corresponding test files
detail.suggestion: Add unit tests for composer helper functions and RAG index/query logic, or integration tests that exercise real (or stubbed) embeddings and indexing

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::unte.summary: Many data-layer CRUD modules lack dedicated tests

evidence: No test files exist for crud_file_bucket, crud_file_object, crud_message, crud_thread, crud_vector_content, crud_vector_store, or crud_vector_store_file
detail.suggestion: Write focused unit tests for each CRUD module using the existing mock_session pattern, or refactor to shared test fixtures that reduce duplication

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::unte.summary: Evaluation runners, metrics, and SDK core modules are untested

evidence: niah_runner.py (412 loc) and qa_runner.py (330 loc) have no tests
All metrics files lack tests
SDK modules llm.py, serve.py, and utils.py have no tests
detail.suggestion: Add unit tests for runner orchestration and metric calculations; add smoke tests for SDK serve and utility functions

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::crud.summary: CRUD unit tests are tightly coupled to Supabase client internals

evidence: test_crud_base.py asserts on mock_session.table(...).insert().execute() call chains
test_crud_api_key.py uses mock_session.table().insert().execute() to verify behavior
Changing the underlying DB client (e.g., from Supabase to Turso) would break these tests even if behavior is identical
detail.suggestion: Refactor CRUD tests to assert on outcomes (return values, raised exceptions) rather than internal Supabase API call sequences, or introduce a repository port/interface to decouple tests from client specifics

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::vect.summary: Vector store integration tests rely on arbitrary sleeps for expiration

evidence: Line 153: time.sleep(1) to wait for vector store expiration
Line 175: time.sleep(1) to wait for vector store expiration
detail.suggestion: Replace fixed sleeps with deterministic expiration triggers (e.g., mock the clock or use a test-specific TTL of 0 with synchronous cleanup)

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::e2e_.summary: E2E background-task test uses fixed polling intervals

evidence: Line 135: time.sleep(2) inside a polling loop waiting for vector store indexing to complete
Test fails if indexing finishes too quickly or too slowly
detail.suggestion: Use an exponential back-off with a short initial delay, or expose a synchronous test hook to await background task completion

review::.::holistic::test_strategy::api_.summary: API config deletion test relies on a fixed sleep for filesystem detection

evidence: Line 119: time.sleep(0.5) waits for the API to detect a deleted config file
detail.suggestion: Replace the sleep with a retry loop that polls the endpoint until the expected empty response is observed, or trigger a config reload explicitly in tests

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: CRUD subclasses use inconsistent filter types: some typed models, some raw dicts

evidence: CRUDAssistant, CRUDVectorStore, and CRUDFileObject override get/list/delete to accept typed filter models (FilterAssistant, FilterVectorStore, FilterFileObject)
CRUDThread, CRUDRun, and CRUDMessage inherit base methods directly and pass raw dict filters like {'id': thread_id}
CRUDAPIKey overrides get/list but still accepts dict | None, not a typed filter model
detail.suggestion: Standardize all CRUD subclasses to use typed filter models that convert to dict internally, or remove typed filter subclasses and consistently use dicts across the layer. Pick one pattern and apply it uniformly.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Session parameter position drifts inconsistently across route handlers

evidence: create_assistant(session, request) → session first
create_thread(request, session) → session last
create_run(thread_id, session, request) → session middle
create_message(thread_id, request, session) → session last
delete_vector_store_file(session, vector_store_id, file_id) → session first (unlike siblings in same file)
detail.suggestion: Adopt a consistent parameter ordering convention for all route handlers, such as path parameters first, then session, then request body. Apply consistently across all router modules.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Public functions return union types based on runtime flags rather than separate endpoints

evidence: Composer.generate_response returns Run | StreamingResponse based on request.stream flag
create_run in runs.py returns Run | StreamingResponse based on runtime branching
chat_complete returns either a ChatCompletionResponse or a StreamingResponse based on req.stream
complete in completions.py similarly branches on req.stream with no return type annotation
detail.suggestion: Either split streaming and non-streaming into separate functions with distinct return types, or document the union return type explicitly and ensure callers handle both cases. Adding return type annotations to chat_complete and complete would also help.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Most route handlers do not document error conditions in docstrings despite raising HTTPExceptions

evidence: create_assistant raises HTTPException with 400 and 500 status codes but docstring only says 'Create an assistant'
create_thread raises HTTPException with 400 and 500 but docstring lacks Raises section
Only modify_assistant in assistants.py includes a Raises section documenting HTTPException conditions
grpc_client.py functions call gRPC stubs without documented failure modes or retry contracts
Broad try/except Exception blocks wrap helper calls and re-raise as 500, but these error contracts are invisible to API consumers reading the docstrings
detail.suggestion: Add Raises sections to all public route handler docstrings documenting the HTTPException conditions (status codes and triggers). Document gRPC client failure modes (connection errors, timeout behavior) in grpc_client.py.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: CRUDBase.delete catches all exceptions and silently returns False

evidence: CRUDBase.delete contains 'except Exception: return False' which hides database connection errors, permission errors, and syntax errors from callers
detail.suggestion: Let specific exceptions propagate or wrap them in a custom CRUD exception that preserves the original cause. Only return False when the delete genuinely matched zero rows.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Public helper module mixes async and sync functions without clear boundary

evidence: async def recv_completion(stream, model) and async def recv_chat(stream, model) are async SSE generators
def grpc_chat_role(role: str) -> lfai.ChatRole, def read_chunks(file: BinaryIO, chunk_size: int), and def object_or_default(obj, _default) are synchronous utilities
All six functions are exported from the same module surface with no subpackage or naming convention separating async from sync utilities
detail.suggestion: Split backend/helpers.py into two modules (e.g., backend/async_helpers.py and backend/sync_helpers.py) or adopt a naming convention that makes async/sync boundary explicit at the import site.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Database client backends expose unequal query surfaces and different factory parameter names

evidence: TursoQueryBuilder adds neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, order, limit, range beyond the QueryBuilder ABC
SupabaseQueryBuilder only wraps the Supabase query object and does not expose the extra filter methods
TursoClient.create(base_url, auth_token) vs SupabaseClient.create(supabase_url, supabase_key) — parameter names differ even though semantic roles are identical
Callers depending on backend-specific query capabilities will fail silently when the factory returns the other backend
detail.suggestion: Expand the QueryBuilder ABC to include the common filter methods, implement them for both backends, and align factory parameter names (e.g., url and key for both). Alternatively, document which query operations are backend-specific.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: Cowabunga tokens router bypasses FastAPI dependency injection used by sibling routers

evidence: count_tokens_route(text: str) hardcodes model config lookup internally instead of accepting model_config: ModelConfig = Depends(get_model_config)
count.py tokens(session, model_config, request) properly injects both session and model_config via Depends
The bypass makes the tokens endpoint less testable and inconsistent with the dependency-injection pattern used across all other route modules
detail.suggestion: Refactor count_tokens_route to accept model_config via Depends(get_model_config), matching the pattern in count.py and the OpenAI routers.

review::.::holistic::api_surface_coheren.summary: SDK serve() and LLM decorator construct interfaces dynamically at runtime

evidence: serve(o, host='0.0.0.0', port=50051) uses hasattr(o, 'ChatComplete') to decide which gRPC services to register
LLM decorator dynamically creates NewClass with ChatComplete, ChatCompleteStream, Complete, CompleteStream, CountTokens methods at runtime
The effective public API of decorated classes is not statically discoverable; type checkers and IDEs cannot infer the generated interface
detail.suggestion: Define an explicit Protocol or ABC that documents the required methods, and use isinstance checks against the protocol instead of hasattr. This makes the contract statically verifiable and improves IDE support for SDK consumers.

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: Cowabunga tokens endpoint lacks authentication while sibling endpoints require it

evidence: tokens.py defines @router.post('/count') async def count_tokens_route(text: str) -> int with no session: Session parameter
All other cowabunga endpoints (auth.py, count.py, vector_stores.py, rag.py) declare session: Session
detail.suggestion: Add session: Session parameter to count_tokens_route to enforce authentication consistent with sibling cowabunga endpoints

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: Cowabunga models endpoint lacks authentication while the equivalent OpenAI endpoint requires it

evidence: models.py defines @router.get('') async def models() with no session: Session parameter
The OpenAI-compliant models.py (src/cowabunga_api/routers/openai/models.py) requires session: Session with comment 'required for authorizing endpoint'
detail.suggestion: Add session: Session parameter to the cowabunga models endpoint for consistency, or document it as intentionally public if that is the design intent

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: API key validation delegates entirely to database RLS without explicit code-level verification

evidence: _validate_api_authorization does 'response = await session.table("api_keys").select("*").execute()' then checks only 'if not response or not response.data'

The function relies on the RLS policy 'Read only if API key matches and is current' to filter results; Python code never verifies the returned key matches the provided key, checks expiration, or validates user identity
detail.suggestion: Explicitly verify the API key in code by comparing the returned record against the parsed key, checking expiration, and confirming the user_id match, rather than assuming RLS is the sole validation layer

review::.::holistic::authorization_consi.summary: Run step endpoints do not verify run belongs to the provided thread_id while sibling endpoints do

evidence: submit_tool_outputs in runs_steps.py does 'run = await crud_run.get(id_=run_id)' without checking run.thread_id == thread_id
cancel_run in runs_steps.py does 'run = await crud_run.get(id_=run_id)' without checking run.thread_id == thread_id
retrieve_run in runs.py filters by both run_id and thread_id: 'await crud_run.get(filters={"id": run_id, "thread_id": thread_id})'
messages.py retrieve_message, modify_message, and delete_message all filter by both message_id and thread_id
detail.suggestion: Update submit_tool_outputs and cancel_run to verify the retrieved run's thread_id matches the path parameter, consistent with runs.py and messages.py patterns

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Multi-line docstrings on trivial one-line getter functions in test utilities

evidence: get_cowabunga_model() has a 6-line docstring for 'return os.getenv("COWABUNGA_MODEL", "vllm")'
get_openai_key() has a 9-line docstring for a 3-line env-var lookup
get_openai_model(), get_cowabunga_api_key(), get_cowabunga_api_url(), get_cowabunga_api_url_base(), openai_client(), cowabunga_client() all repeat the same pattern
detail.suggestion: Replace verbose Google-style docstrings on trivial getters with single-line docstrings or omit them entirely; the types and behavior are self-evident.

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Meaningless 'except Exception as e: raise e' blocks in CRUD base methods

evidence: create() wraps result parsing in try/except that catches Exception and immediately re-raises it without modification
list() and update() repeat the same pattern
The only genuine handler is the IndexError branch in get(); the bare 'except Exception as e: raise e' adds no value and obscures the real control flow
detail.suggestion: Remove the no-op except/raise wrappers; keep only the IndexError guard in get().

review::.::holistic::ai_generated_debt::.summary: Identical copy-pasted try/except → HTTPException boilerplate across OpenAI router files

evidence: assistants.py create_assistant: 'except Exception as exc: raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail="Unable to parse assistant request") from exc'

detail.suggestion: Define a UserResponse dataclass or TypedDict in base.py and narrow execute() to return a generic result type. Replace Any with the concrete types that each implementation actually returns.

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: MetadataObject class defined twice identically in the same file

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: vllm package uses src/ subdirectory while all sibling packages are flat

repeater, whisper, text-embeddings, and llama-cpp-python place main.py directly at package root
text-embeddings and llama-cpp-python place model_download.py under scripts/ rather than src/

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: CRUDFileBucket and CRUDVectorContent do not inherit from CRUDBase and use divergent method signatures

evidence: CRUDFileBucket defines its own __init__(db, model) with upload/download/delete instead of create/get/list/update/delete

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: CorrectnessMetric extends GEval while sibling metrics extend BaseMetric

annotation_relevancy.py and niah_metrics.py inherit from BaseMetric and implement measure(), a_measure(), is_successful(), and __name__ property manually

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: Turso database modules use dataclasses while the rest of the codebase uses pydantic BaseModel

evidence: turso_auth.py defines User, UserResponse, and Session as @dataclass
turso_client.py defines TursoResult as @dataclass
All API typedef modules and the database base config use pydantic BaseModel with no documented rationale for the dataclass exception

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: thread_create.py uses Optional[List[...]] instead of modern pipe-union syntax

The rest of the codebase consistently uses pipe unions such as list[Message] | None and dict | None

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: supabase_session.py is a dependency module placed in the routers directory

evidence: supabase_session.py defines Session = Annotated[AsyncClient, Depends(...)] and auth helper functions but does not declare an APIRouter
All other top-level files in routers/ (base.py, health.py) and all subdirectories (cowabunga/, openai/) define APIRouter instances and route handlers

review::.::holistic::convention_outlier:.summary: LLM class decorator uses PascalCase instead of snake_case

evidence: llm.py defines 'def LLM(_cls):' used as @LLM decorator on Model classes
Python convention uses snake_case for function decorators (e.g., @classmethod, @property, @lru_cache)

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Pointless try/except wrappers in CRUD base add no error context

evidence: crud_base.py:41 'except Exception as e: raise e' around model construction
crud_base.py:61 'except Exception as e: raise e' around get()
crud_base.py:80 'except Exception as e: raise e' around list()
crud_base.py:100 'except Exception as e: raise e' around update()
crud_base.py:115 'except Exception: return False' around delete()
crud_vector_content.py:56 'except Exception as e: raise e'
crud_api_key.py:118 'except Exception as exc: raise exc'
detail.suggestion: Remove the pointless try/except wrappers. If error translation is needed, catch specific exceptions (e.g., IndexError, ValidationError) and raise a domain-specific exception with context. If not needed, let exceptions propagate naturally.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Exception context lost in count router due to missing 'from' clause

evidence: count.py:33-34 'except Exception as e: raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Error counting tokens: {str(e)}")' — no 'from e', so the original traceback and exception type are lost
detail.suggestion: Add 'from e' to the HTTPException raise to preserve the original exception chain for debugging.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Broad except masks HTTPException and re-raises with wrong detail

evidence: assistant_types.py:146-150 catches 'except Exception:' (which includes HTTPException from crud_vector_store.get) and re-raises 'HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid vector store id was provided")', hiding the original 404 or 500
detail.suggestion: Catch specific exceptions expected from the CRUD call, or catch HTTPException separately and re-raise it unchanged. Only catch broad Exception for unexpected errors and log them.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Bare except silently swallows JWT decode failures

evidence: turso_auth.py:247-251 'try: jwt.decode(...); except: pass' — a bare except swallows all errors including jwt.ExpiredSignatureError, jwt.InvalidTokenError, and even KeyboardInterrupt
detail.suggestion: Catch specific jwt exceptions (jwt.DecodeError, jwt.ExpiredSignatureError) and log the failure instead of silently ignoring it.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: file_ids_are_valid catches all exceptions and returns False

evidence: index.py:345-349 'try: ... except Exception: return False' — any error (including AttributeError, TypeError, or database connection failures) is converted to False, making real bugs invisible
detail.suggestion: Catch only the expected exception from the CRUD get() call (e.g., IndexError or a domain-specific NotFoundError). Let unexpected exceptions propagate so they surface as bugs.

review::.::holistic::error_consistency::.summary: Data layer mixes raise, return None, return False, and Result-type error strategies

evidence: crud_base.py: create() raises on Exception, get() returns None on IndexError, delete() returns False on Exception
crud_run.py: update() returns None when response is empty instead of raising
turso_client.py: execute() returns TursoResult(data=[], error=str(e)) instead of raising, diverging from Supabase client behavior
detail.suggestion: Choose a single error strategy for the data layer: either raise domain exceptions for all failures, or consistently return a Result type. Do not mix both across sibling modules.

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::get.summary: get_initial_messages_base and get_ending_messages_base construct event strings rather than retrieve existing data

evidence: get_initial_messages_base builds SSE event strings from scratch using from_assistant_stream_event_to_str(ThreadRunCreated(...))
get_ending_messages_base similarly constructs a ThreadRunCompleted event string from scratch
detail.suggestion: Rename to build_initial_messages_base and build_ending_messages_base to accurately reflect their construction behavior

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::gen.summary: ConfigHandler.process() uses a generic verb that obscures its config-reloading intent

evidence: def process(self, event) handles created/modified/deleted file events by calling load_config_file or remove_model_by_config
detail.suggestion: Rename to _handle_config_file_event or _reload_config_on_event to communicate the actual behavior

review::.::holistic::naming_quality::inf.summary: _to_list_of_strs uses informal abbreviation inconsistent with full-word conventions

evidence: def _to_list_of_strs(v: list) -> list[str]: uses 'strs' instead of 'strings'
detail.suggestion: Rename to _to_list_of_strings to match the codebase's preference for full words

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: object_or_default is a generic helper that restates a ternary None check without added policy

evidence: backend/helpers.py line 121: 'def object_or_default(obj: Any | None, _default: Any) -> Any: if obj is not None: return obj else: return _default'
Used 9 times in routers/openai/assistants.py lines 141-159 for name, description, instructions, model, tools, tool_resources, temperature, top_p, metadata, response_format
detail.suggestion: Inline the expression: use 'request.name or old_assistant.name' where falsy-vs-None distinction does not matter, or 'request.name if request.name is not None else old_assistant.name' where it does. Then remove object_or_default and its tests.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: MetadataObject is a schema-less dynamic bag that accepts arbitrary kwargs and returns None for missing attributes

evidence: typedef/common.py: class MetadataObject defines __init__(self, **kwargs) and __getattr__(self, key) returning self.__dict__.get(key)
backend/converters.py line 101-103: metadata=MetadataObject(vector_ids=all_vector_ids.__str__) — only production usage
detail.suggestion: Replace MetadataObject with a typed dataclass (e.g., MessageMetadata) that declares the vector_ids field, or pass a plain dict if the consumer accepts it.

review::.::holistic::abstraction_fitness.summary: get_session is a pure backward-compatibility alias for create_database_client with no deprecation plan

evidence: database_factory.py line 57-66: async def get_session() -> DatabaseClient: return await create_database_client()
Docstring claims backward compatibility but there is no @deprecated marker, no warnings.warn, and no tracked sunset plan
detail.suggestion: Add a DeprecationWarning to get_session, migrate any remaining callers to create_database_client(), and remove the alias in the next cleanup cycle.

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: pyPDF2 declared but never used in cowabunga_evals

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: seaborn declared but never used in cowabunga_evals

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: python-dotenv declared twice in same pyproject.toml

evidence: python-dotenv == 1.0.1 appears on both line 13 and line 20 of the dependencies array
detail.suggestion: Remove the duplicate python-dotenv entry

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: toml and PyYAML imported directly but not declared in cowabunga_api dependencies

evidence: config.py imports toml and yaml directly, but neither package appears in cowabunga_api/pyproject.toml dependencies; they only work via transitive inclusion
detail.suggestion: Add toml and PyYAML as explicit dependencies in cowabunga_api/pyproject.toml

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: transformers version conflict between root and whisper package

evidence: Root pyproject.toml dev-whisper specifies transformers[torch] == 4.45.1, while packages/whisper/pyproject.toml dev specifies transformers[torch] == 4.39.3

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: Both dompurify and isomorphic-dompurify in UI production dependencies

review::.::holistic::dependency_health::.summary: aiostream declared but never used in vllm package

detail.suggestion: Remove aiostream from packages/vllm/pyproject.toml dependencies

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: LLM decorator is a 215-line monolith with inline class and nested closures

evidence: The LLM() decorator function spans lines 50-265 and defines an entire NewClass inline with 7 async methods
create_chat_completion_response and create_completion_response are 25-line nested functions that differ only in response type, yet are not parameterized
ChatComplete, ChatCompleteStream, Complete, and CompleteStream share nearly identical finish-reason and token-counting logic that is duplicated 4 times
detail.suggestion: Extract NewClass to module level, parameterize the response-factory helpers, and extract shared finish-reason/token-counting logic into a single helper method.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: run_experiment is a ~140-line monolith mixing I/O, API calls, scoring, and cleanup

evidence: run_experiment contains a tight loop over self.niah_data that writes temp files, uploads via API, creates assistants, creates threads, runs evals, scores results, and mutates instance state (self.current_file, self.current_assistant)
In-place dataset mutation via self.niah_data.add_column() is scattered at the end of the method after the loop
The method body interleaves file-system I/O, network I/O, scoring arithmetic, and logging without clear phase boundaries
detail.suggestion: Extract per-row evaluation into a _evaluate_single_row() method, separate setup/teardown phases from the scoring loop, and move dataset augmentation out of the experiment method.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Identical 16-line tool-resources lookup duplicated in sync and stream composer methods

evidence: generate_message_for_thread lines 179-195 and stream_generate_message_for_thread lines 249-264 contain verbatim identical CRUDAssistant lookup and tool_resources resolution logic
Both branches perform the same fallback chain: check passed tool_resources -> query assistant -> validate type -> model_validate dump -> else None
detail.suggestion: Extract a private _resolve_tool_resources(request, session, tool_resources) method that both sync and stream variants can call.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: User-construction SQL and object building is duplicated across three authentication methods

evidence: _authenticate_api_key (lines 132-151), _authenticate_jwt (lines 163-182), and _authenticate_session (lines 211-230) each contain nearly identical SELECT * FROM users queries and User(...) constructor calls
The only varying parts are the token-lookup queries; user fetching and validation are identical
detail.suggestion: Extract a _fetch_active_user(db, user_id) -> User helper that centralizes the user query, existence check, and User construction.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Dataclass types defined inside a method body, recreated on every call

evidence: TursoAuthClient.get_user defines UserResponse and User dataclasses at lines 234-243 inside the async method body
These types are recreated at runtime on every get_user() call and are not local to the method's control flow
detail.suggestion: Move UserResponse and User dataclass definitions to module level alongside the existing TursoResult dataclass.

review::.::holistic::low_level_elegance:.summary: Async helper functions defined inside a method rather than extracted

evidence: request_checks_and_modifications (lines 88-201) defines new_vector_store_from_file_ids and attach_existing_vector_store_from_id as nested async functions inside the method body
The method is 113 lines long; extracting the two helpers would reduce it to ~40 lines of orchestration logic
detail.suggestion: Extract new_vector_store_from_file_ids and attach_existing_vector_store_from_id to private methods on the class or module-level helpers.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: CRUD layer type-hints Supabase AsyncClient instead of generic DatabaseClient

evidence: CRUDBase.__init__ accepts db: AsyncClient (supabase-specific) rather than DatabaseClient
TursoClient implements DatabaseClient but cannot be used by existing CRUDs due to mismatched typing
database_factory.py creates DatabaseClient but callers cast or ignore the abstraction
detail.suggestion: Refactor all CRUD constructors and CRUDBase to accept DatabaseClient, relying on the QueryBuilder interface for queries.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Composer backend module imports chat completion functions from the router layer

evidence: composer.py imports chat_complete and chat_complete_stream_raw from cowabunga_api.routers.openai.chat
This creates a reverse dependency where backend depends on router, violating layer direction
detail.suggestion: Extract chat completion logic into a backend service module (e.g., backend/chat_service.py) and have both composer.py and the chat router depend on it.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Backend Composer raises FastAPI HTTPExceptions directly, coupling backend to HTTP transport

evidence: Composer.list_messages raises HTTPException(status_code=500) on CRUD failure
Backend layer should not know about HTTP status codes
detail.suggestion: Introduce backend-specific exceptions (e.g., BackendError) and map them to HTTPExceptions in the router layer.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Run router prints raw traceback before wrapping exceptions, bypassing structured logging at the router-backend seam

evidence: runs.py catches Exception from Composer and calls traceback.print_exc() before raising HTTPException
This loses structured log context and makes error monitoring unpredictable
detail.suggestion: Replace traceback.print_exc() with logger.exception() and re-raise HTTPException preserving the original cause.

review::.::holistic::mid_level_elegance:.summary: Vector store router mixes high-level service calls with direct CRUD calls

evidence: create_vector_store and modify_vector_store delegate to IndexingService
retrieve_vector_store, list_vector_store_files, and delete_vector_store_file instantiate CRUDs directly
This creates an inconsistent handoff pattern across the router-data seam
detail.suggestion: Consolidate all vector store operations behind a single service facade so the router always delegates to one collaborator.
